text: Managing a team effectively requires a balance of several key skills. First, clear communication is essential so that everyone understands their roles and the team's goals. Next, delegation involves assigning tasks based on each member's strengths, which helps improve efficiency and trust. Providing regular feedback is also important; it should be constructive and focused on growth, not just criticism. To keep the team motivated, recognize achievements and create a positive work environment. Finally, conflict resolution skills help address disagreements quickly and fairly. By listening to all sides and finding common ground, you can prevent small issues from becoming big problems. Together, these practices build a strong, productive team.
